channel.title: 中国日报-文化
channel.description: 中国日报-文化 RSS Feed

item.title: Shandong Art Troupe delights Cubans with New Year greetings
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: With festive Chinese music and dances, acrobatics, magic shows and martial arts performances, the Shandong Art Troupe staged an exciting show during the 27th Havana International Book Fair in the Cuban capital of Havana on Feb 2.

item.title: Shaanxi museum releases calendar of cultural relics
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The Shaanxi History Museum released the first calendar of cultural relics in Shaanxi province on Tuesday, showcasing 365 pieces of exquisite collections from ancient China.

item.title: Gaming industry opens a whole new chapter
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Zhang Wenxiu loved reading Zhu Xian, or Jade Dynasty, the smash-hit supernatural novel which created a stir online.

item.title: The plot thickens for online literary companies
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Stories from aspiring authors are now read by millions of users on the internet before being turned into movies, TV series and games.

item.title: Playlist
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Television viewers and those familiar with people on the autism spectrum agree: The Good Doctor is a good show.

item.title: UIBE hosts talks on global affairs
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The University of International Business and Economics hosted its International Dialogue 2017 Annual Convention in Beijing over Nov 25-26.

item.title: Celebrating 10 years of building links
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Organized by the Confucius Institute Headquarters in Beijing, the Chinese Bridge Chinese-proficiency contests are three annual competitions for foreign college students, foreign secondary school students and other foreign learners around the world.

item.title: Foreigners share their 'China story'
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Overseas students at a Xi'an college feel at home

item.title: Cultural connection
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Chinese Bridge Chinese-proficiency competitions are bringing people together and helping to build a worldwide community working for a shared future. Fang Aiqing reports.

item.title: Largest Pan Tianshou exhibition opens in Hangzhou
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: An Ethos of Fortitude - The Exhibition in Commemoration of Pan Tianshou's 120th Anniversary, opened Friday at the Zhejiang Art Museum in Hangzhou, East China's Zhejiang province. The exhibition is the largest presentation of Pan's works, with over 120 pieces of his artwork and manuscripts.

item.title: Zong Qixiang's family donates 71 paintings to NAMOC
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The National Art Museum of China (NAMOC) in Beijing recently received a donation of 71 paintings created by Zong Qixiang (1917-99), an established painter and educator, from his family.

item.title: Exhibition celebrates Suzhou artists from Ming Dynasty on
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Six Centuries of Art, an exhibition on now at the National Art Museum of China in Beijing through Sunday, reviews artistic creations since the Ming Dynasty (1368-1644) of the historic city of Suzhou and its neighboring areas in East China's Jiangsu province.

item.title: Wei Jie's global speech tour arrives in Hangzhou
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: A speech, created with great effort by Wei Jie, is different from the magic speech by Steve Jobs or Luo Yonghao's speech. It is the culmination of wisdom lasting thousands of years and the accumulation of many ancient civilizations.

item.title: Ancient wine vessels from Palace Museum's collection
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: In celebration of winter, the Palace Museum in Beijing has shared a range of photos featuring its collection of wine vessels with netizens on the micro blog Sina Weibo.

item.title: Special auction featuring street art kicks off in HK
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Sotheby's is the world's fourth oldest auction house in continuous operation. Hong Kong Sotheby's auction agency also has long enjoyed a good reputation. Preview exhibition of "Borderless: Temporary Art" and first special auction featuring street art was held by Sotheby's in Hong Kong on Monday. The auction on Dec 8 will be divided into two sessions.

item.title: Govt, Baidu to jointly create 2,000 online museums
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The State Administration of Cultural Heritage and internet giant Baidu signed cooperation agreement on Monday in Wuzhen, Zhejiang province, to have a better online platform introducing cultural heritage.

item.title: Creative designs from key art schools on display in Dalian
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: More than 300 artworks created by students from key art universities are being displayed at the art museum of LuXun Academy of Fine Arts to showcase creative ideas and skills of China's young generation. Items on display range from Chinese paintings and calligraphies to sculptures, porcelains and industry designs.

item.title: Culture center adds sparkle to Wucun's attractions
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: A new culture center is adding charm to Wucun's agrarian allure of green fields and white-walled farmhouses with black-shingled rooftops.

item.title: Obscure no more: Ancient female painters' works go on display
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Some lucky ancient Chinese female painters had their works well preserved. Sixty such works went on display at the Zhejiang Provincial Museum on Nov 25.

item.title: Sizhou inkstone carving in Guizhou
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Sizhou inkstone, mostly produced in Cengong county, has a history of thousands of years.

item.title: China to turn Marquis of Haihun tomb into cultural relics park
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Chinese authorities have approved a plan to turn the tomb of the Marquis of Haihun into a national-level archeological relics park.

item.title: Chinese elements highlight 2017 Wellington Christmas parade
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: New Zealand's capital Wellington ushered in its annual Christmas season with celebratory events on Nov 25-26. The two-day festival featured a grand Santa parade in which nearly 200 performers participated, attracting over 20,000 people packing the Lambton Quay to watch.

item.title: Nanjing ushers in sand painting competition
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Sand painting is also called "art on the fingertips". Artists use sand to create marvelous pictures accompanied by light and music. The first international sand painting competition featuring idyllic life was held in Nanjing, East China's Jiangsu province, on Dec 3.

item.title: Calabash carving shows Xi'an culture
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Liang Ping'an, a calabash carving artist living in Xi'an, turns this ordinary vegetable into handicrafts demonstrating the history and culture of this time-honored city.

item.title: Chinese rappers to go on a countrywide tour
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Chinese rappers join a national tour, the Door & Key Hip-Hop Tour, which kicks off from Beijing's Cadillac Arena on Dec 3.

item.title: 12 spots given national-level archaeological park status
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Twelve spots were newly inscribed onto the list of national-level archaeological site park, which was released by the State Administration of Cultural Heritage (SACH), on Saturday.

item.title: CUC to usher in New Year with cultural awards ceremony
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The School of Economics and Management at Communication University of China (CUC) will hold a cultural awards ceremony by the end of this year to promote the country's cultural industries.

item.title: National treasures come alive on latest TV show
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: A large-scale cultural exploration program National Treasure made its debut on Channel 3 of China Central Television on Sunday night.

item.title: New stills of 'Youth' released
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: New stills of 'Youth' released

item.title: 4th Silk Road International Film Festival concludes in Fujian
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Chinese actor Jacky Chan attends the closing ceremony of the 4th Silk Road International Film Festival in Fuzhou city, East China's Fujian province, Dec 3, 2017.

item.title: 'Big Fish and Begonia' wins at international animation festival
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The animation by Chinese director Liang Xuan and Zhang Chun "Big Fish and Begonia" won the first prize of the full-length film category of the 15th Anilogue International Animation Festival here on Dec 3.

item.title: Acrobats inject life into folk art
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Born in a county where more than 20,000 people are skilled acrobats, Yin Bo has spent most of his life performing around the country.

item.title: Hello Teddy!
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The world's largest teddy bear show kicked off in Moscow on Dec 1. Thousands of cuddly teddy bears made by hundreds of artists from Europe and Asia are on show at the 9th Moscow International Exhibition of Collectible Teddy Bears. Along with adorable bears, there are also various workshops and demonstrations of plush bear-making.

item.title: Tibetan art: Tangka painting
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Tangka, a style of Tibetan art that involves painting on embroidery, has been liked by tourists thanks to a thriving development of tourism in Tibet, where over 2,000 Tangka artists can create about 1,000 pieces of Tangka paintings every year.

item.title: Architect of stilted house of Tujia people
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The 70-year-old Tan Zunzhu is an architect of stilted house and he built more than 200 stilted houses in his 55-year career.

item.title: Confucius Institute in Cuba celebrates 8th anniversary
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The Confucius Institute of Havana celebrated its eighth anniversary on Thursday.

item.title: Liverpool hosts months-long festival of Chinese art, culture
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The British city of Liverpool is to host a nine-month-long festival of the best of Chinese contemporary art and culture next year to mark the city's 10th anniversary as the 2008 European Capital of Culture, city hall officials announced on Thursday.

item.title: Chinese giant lanterns illuminate Edinburgh Zoo
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Edinburgh Zoo is hosting the lone Chinese giant lantern show in Scotland, illuminating the cold nights with warmth and colorful lights during the festive season.

item.title: Best bets
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Since making their full-length debut with 2012's multiplatinum Night Visions, Imagine Dragons have drawn worldwide adoration for their anthemic yet moody alt-rock.

item.title: Chinese production set to create history on Broadway
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Shanghai Hengyuanxiang (HYX) Drama Development Company and Nederlander Worldwide Entertainment signed a deal on Nov 6 that would see Shimmer become the first Chinese musical to have an openended run on Broadway.

item.title: Shanghai loves musicals
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: A slew of famous Broadway productions will be hitting town next year as theaters seek to satisfy a growing appetite for foreign shows.

item.title: Recording history
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Hou Bo, born in 1924, is a Chinese photographer who was among the best known photographers of Mao Zedong. She died in Beijing at 10 pm on Nov 26 at the age of 93.

item.title: Bangladesh is obsessed with Chinese traditional 'wushu'
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Considered a living legend, Alamgir Shah Bhuiyan, is the highest ranking wushu master in Bangladesh.

item.title: Crafting a future for traditional arts
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: As the traditional arts enjoy something of a renaissance, innovation remained the buzzword at the recent World Leisure Expo in Hangzhou.

item.title: Slovak sax quartet makes China debut
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Slovak saxophone quartet Saxophone Syncopators made their debut show in China by performing at the capital's Minzu Theater on Dec 1.

item.title: Students learn shell paintings in school of Jiangxi
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Combo photo taken on Nov 29, 2017 shows the shell paintings created by teachers and students of Huyun Middle School in Huyun township of Wannian county, East China's Jiangxi province.

item.title: Foreigners experience Chinese fan art in Suzhou
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Twenty-three foreign teachers and students from Tsinghua University have come to Suzhou Arts & Crafts Museum to experience the fan art.

item.title: A county where ducks tend to organic rice paddies
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Every restaurant is remembered by its signature dish, while the rice is always forgotten.

item.title: Pasta event adds twist to Italian food week
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Click, click, click. As I walked into the grand ballroom of an upscale hotel in downtown Beijing, three women at the entrance frantically tapped the counters in their hands.

item.title: Cutting-edge knife skills
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The Huaiyang dish Wensi Tofu gained a sharp reputation due to the intricate knife skills it requires thanks to A Bite of China, a popular documentary series about Chinese food.

item.title: 'Marquis of Haihun' tomb set for security upgrade
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The security and protection system of China's most complete Western Han Dynasty (206 BC- 25 AD) cemetery and its surrounding areas will be upgraded.

item.title: Tomb frescos go on display in Shanghai
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: A total of 89 frescos from ancient tombs, divided into 12 groups, are being displayed at an exhibition unveiled at the Shanghai Museum on Wednesday.

item.title: 'Creative lab' fuses tradition and technology
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The Palace Museum, aka the Forbidden City, joined hands with Chinese internet giant Tencent to establish a "creative laboratory" in Beijing on Wednesday.

item.title: Exhibition shows exported porcelain
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Chinese porcelain was exported from the 16th century and decorated the homes of royalty and dignitaries â and, eventually, ordinary people.

item.title: Chinese singer releases an album reflecting on the past decade
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Wang Xi gave himself the English name Elvis to pay homage to his music idol, Elvis Presley.

item.title: Building Images
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Zhang Qianxi waited by a swimming pool at Tianjin University on a cold winter afternoon last year. At a certain point when the light reflected perfectly on the water, he clicked the shutter.

item.title: Five centuries of art to go under hammer
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The Palace Museum in Beijing has mounted several blockbuster exhibitions of classical Chinese paintings and calligraphy over the past two years. Thanks to successful publicity campaigns, word about these shows went viral on social media, making it the most effective means for drawing visitors.

item.title: 'Discover The Forbidden City' in Sydney
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: In recent years, with the more frequent international exchange and increasing innovation, "the Palace Museum culture" has become a hit. "Discover The Forbidden City", an interactive experience activity sponsored by China Cultural Centre in Sydney and the Palace Museum, was held at four local schools in Sydney from Nov 21 to Nov 22. The event aimed to increase local students' understanding of Chinese culture through various activities.

item.title: 'Silk Road Landscape Map' donated to the Palace Museum
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The Palace Museum received the painting Silk Road Landscape Map, which was donated by Xu Rongmao, founder and chairman of Hong Kong Shimao Group, during a ceremony in the Palace Museum Nov 30. Xu bought the painting for $20 million from a Japanese collector.

item.title: Art education platform founded
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Guardian Education Center has launched an international-education platform to offer short- and long-term courses on art history, collecting, archaeology and business in Beijing.

item.title: Craftsman creates vivid clay sculptures
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Ding Jianhua, the 44-year-old man from Hangzhou city of Zhejiang province, makes clay sculptures.

item.title: Online celebrity&nbsp;animals of 2017
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: This hilarious image shows red squirrels displaying their musical skills on mini musical instruments. The critters looked like they could be driving their woodland neighbors nuts with violins, guitars, saxophones and even a little drum kit.

item.title: Chinese actress Liu Yifei to play Mulan in Disney's live-action adaptation
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Chinese actress Liu Yifei is set to star as the titular female warrior in Disney's live-action adaptation of the classic Chinese epic, Mulan.

item.title: Huayi Brothers' big plans for small screen
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Huayi Brothers Media Corp, a major factor in the rise of some of China's most popular film celebrities like director Feng Xiaogang and actress Li Bingbing, recently announced plans to expand its influence among television viewers.

item.title: Centuries-old pingshu back in spotlight
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The centuries-old art form of pingshu, or storytelling, is making a welcome return to television after a long hiatus. Xu Fan reports.

item.title: Shunji Iwai's film based on real-life incidents
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Japanese filmmaker Shunji Iwai still clearly remembers an afternoon when he was in second grade.

item.title: Huayi Brothers' big plans for small screen
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Huayi Brothers Media Corp, a major factor in the rise of some of China's most popular film celebrities like director Feng Xiaogang and actress Li Bingbing, recently announced plans to expand its influence among television viewers.

item.title: Back in spotlight
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The centuries-old art form of pingshu, or storytelling, is making a welcome return to television after a long hiatus. Xu Fan reports.

item.title: Fashion event for diplomats takes center stage
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Diplomats and their families from 15 embassies in China dressed in their countries' traditional costumes during the 7th "GLOBALCARE" Diplomat National Costume Competition held in Beijing on Sunday. The event is designed to highlight native fashions and to break the stereotype that diplomats are always in suits.

item.title: Sculptures, other works by noted Rodin peer go on show
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Antoine Bourdelle (1861-1929), a noted French sculptor, painter and educator, spent some 15 years assisting Auguste Rodin in his studio. He was chiefly influenced by Rodin's artistic views while he also explored his own style, finding inspiration in classical Greek and Romanesque art.

item.title: New panel to promote art and science
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Art and science play a vital role in society, making people to think and to create. And in a fast changing world, art and science are even more crucial.

item.title: Li Keran works to go under the block
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The 1970s and '80s saw ink-brush master Li Keran (1907-89) pushing his creation of landscapes to a peak. Three of his paintings which were created during this period of time will be auctioned at Poly's major autumn sales from Dec 16 to 20 in Beijing.

item.title: Exhibition of socialist realism paintings opens in Shanghai
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Zhao Wangyun (1906-77) co-founded the Chang'an School of Painting in the 1960s with two other ink-brush artists based in Xi'an in Northwestern China. They sought to revive the tradition of Chinese ink art through the depiction of the everyday lives of common people.

item.title: Paintings feature the natural beauty of Beijing
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: About 200 artists went to Yanqing district in Beijing and were inspired by the beauty there. Their works, 90 paintings, will be displayed on Nov 30 at the National Art Museum of China.

item.title: Broadway musical 'Legally Blonde' to be staged in Beijing
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The Broadway musical Legally Blonde will be staged in Beijing from Jan 10 to 14 at the Poly Theater.

item.title: Insight into Nobel Prize in Literature
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Kjell Espmark, former chairman of the Nobel Committee for Literature and life member of the Swedish Academy, made his ninth trip to China and shared his insights on the Nobel Prize with audiences in Beijing.

item.title: Revealing history of Chinese lingerie
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Revealing history of Chinese lingerie

item.title: Shanghai stages street art exhibition on fallen leaves
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: A street art exhibition on fallen leaves is currently underway at Yueyang Road in Shanghai., which is themed on "love".

item.title: Fu Baoshi painting auctioned for nearly HK$205m
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: An ink-brush figure painting by modern master Fu Baoshi (1904-65) grossed HK$204.85 million ($26 million) at a Christie's auction Tuesday in Hong Kong.

item.title: Playlist
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Before founding Bully, guitarist and songwriter Alicia Bognanno interned at Chicago's Electrical Audio Studio for Steve Albini, the alternative rock engineer who worked with such 1990s heavy hitters as Nirvana and the Pixies.

item.title: Revealing history of Chinese lingerie
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Chinese women, especially ancient ones, had more comfortable and high-quality choices that would not allow them to doubt if their body was perfect or not.

item.title: Forbidden City shares tech ideas with other museums
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Experts from around the world shared their experiences adapting new technologies to museum operations at a forum in the Palace Museum, or Forbidden City, in Beijing.

item.title: Chinese directors visit Hollywood
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: China Filmmaker Summit, a program organized by the State Administration of Press, Publication, Radio, Film and Television, China's top sectoral regulator, and the Hollywood studio 20th Century Fox, was recently held in the United States where some rising Chinese directors viewed advanced filming technologies.

item.title: Miner's story inspires film
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Award-winning actor Duan Yihong has recently become a familiar face in crime thrillers.

item.title: Exhibition displays paintings from 600 years ago
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: A total of 164 paintings by artists from Suzhou, East China's Jiangsu province, will go on display at the National Art Museum of China from Nov 28 to Dec 10.

item.title: All-star cast assembled for Guo Degang's directorial debut
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: A dream cast might not be available to most newbie directors, but cross-talk comedian Guo Degang has assembled a cast of up to 33 stars for his directorial debut, The Face of Gene.

item.title: Crime thriller 'Manhunt' brings three firsts for Fukuyama
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Japanese star Masaharu Fukuyama once enthralled Chinese fans with his renditions of songs in Tokyo Love Story, the most popular Japanese TV series airing on the Chinese mainland during the mid-1990s.

item.title: Jimei Arles International Photo Festival kicks off in SE China
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The 2017 Jimei Arles International Photo Festival kicked off on Saturday in southeastern China's coastal city Xiamen.

item.title: Peking Opera inherited in primary school
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Students at Guangraolu Primary School in Qingdao city of Shandong province have fallen in love with a Peking Opera learning activity held once a week.

item.title: Ming Dynasty jar fetches $27.4 m at auction
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: A 16th-century polychrome jar that once adorned Chinese imperial palaces fetched HK$213.9 million ($27.4 million) at Christie's auction on Monday night.

item.title: Carved candles light up Christmas season
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Famous candle-carving artists Alla and Anastasia Pavlenko, mother and daughter, exhibited over a hundred carved candles that day in Hong Kong. A focal point of this exhibition was the duplicates of the "royal candles" which had once been sent to Queen Elizabeth II to celebrate her 90th birthday. In addition, as exhibited in Hong Kong, customized candles with Chinese elements were included.

item.title: Real estate to canvas
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Powerlong Museum, among the latest additions to Shanghai's private art destinations, stands alone in the metropolis' far southwestern Minhang district. Many other such institutions, such as the Long Museum, Yuz Museum and Shanghai Center of Photography, are found in the well-known West Bund area, closer to the city center.

item.title: Real estate to canvas
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Powerlong Museum, among the latest additions to Shanghai's private art destinations, stands alone in the metropolis' far southwestern Minhang district. Many other such institutions, such as the Long Museum, Yuz Museum and Shanghai Center of Photography, are found in the well-known West Bund area, closer to the city center.

item.title: Zhang Yanzi's ongoing show on herbs, happiness and healing
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Two years ago, Chinese painter Zhang Yanzi traveled to the Amazon rainforest in Brazil, and found the local brew ayahuasca, which is made from plants and is said to have hallucinogenic properties. The drink is used in spiritual medicine there.

item.title: The first China-Indonesia film project launched in Jakarta
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The first China-Indonesia co-produced film project Tsunami was initiated Nov 27 in Jakarta, capital of Indonesia.

item.title: Fashion design in Mongolian style
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The 14th Mongolian Costume Arts Festival kicked off in Hohhot, North China's Inner Mongolia autonomous region, on Nov 27.

item.title: Former banker returns home to promote Miao embroidery
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Chen Guotao, born in a Miao ethnic family in the mountains of Yaoyang, Southwest China's Chongqing, successfully made her way to the big cities. But the love for the Miao embroidery made her quit the highly paid job at a State-owned bank and returned home to promote the folk art.

item.title: Chinese cabbage-themed relics from Palace Museum's collection
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: In celebration of winter, the Palace Museum in Beijing has shared a range of photos featuring its collection of Chinese cabbage-themed relics with netizens on the micro blog Sina Weibo.

item.title: Real estate to canvas
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Powerlong Museum, among the latest additions to Shanghai's private art destinations, stands alone in the metropolis' far southwestern Minhang district. Many other such institutions, such as the Long Museum, Yuz Museum and Shanghai Center of Photography, are found in the well-known West Bund area, closer to the city center.

item.title: Zhang Yanzi's ongoing show on herbs, happiness and healing
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Two years ago, Chinese painter Zhang Yanzi traveled to the Amazon rainforest in Brazil, and found the local brew ayahuasca, which is made from plants and is said to have hallucinogenic properties. The drink is used in spiritual medicine there.

item.title: Dramatic effect
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Propelled by the success of Wolf Warrior 2 - this year's fifth-highest-grossing movie worldwide - China's box-office takings have exceeded 50 billion yuan ($7.58 billion) for the first time, a historic annual high.

item.title: Female embroiderers compete in Guizhou
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: A Buyi embroidery cultural product creative contest was held Saturday at Nafu Square in Ceheng county, Guizhou province, featuring activities from the 2017 Buyi Cultural Year.

item.title: Summit on traditional villages held in Guizhou
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The third China Traditional Village Qiandongnan Summit was held in Yingshang village, Xijiang town, Leishan county, Guizhou province from Nov 25 to 26.

item.title: Transforming leaves into artwork
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The creative arts students at Communication University of Shanxi changed leaves into colorful paintings.

item.title: Hungarian artisans share traditions with visitors in Beijing
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: As part of its Christmas celebrations, the Beijing Hungarian Cultural Institute is holding a series of events to ramp up the holiday spirit.

item.title: Intangible cultural heritage promotes fruit deals
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Luoguzaxi, a traditional style of drama accompanied by the sound of gongs and drums, is a local intangible cultural heritage originating in Linyi county, North China's Shanxi province. In order to celebrate the apple promotions in Beijing and the sixth fruit festival in Linyi county, folk artists were organized to performLuoguzaxiin Beijing Nov 26.

item.title: 'Sky In Their Eyes' debuts late on friday
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: A music and dance performance, called "Sky In Their Eyes: The Antelope in a Vanishing Landscape", had been performed live for the very first time on the night of November 24 at the Raphael Room at London's famous V and A museum as part of Friday Late: Sino Flux, a celebration of the contemporary art, design, sounds and states of China.

item.title: Vivid wicker-made animals on show in Shandong
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The Fourth China (Linshu) Wickerwork Trade Fair is becoming a "zoo", with the wicker crafts on display resembling a variety of animals.

item.title: Explore cloisonne enamel factory in Beijing
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Cloisonne is an ancient technique for metalwork decoration using glasslike enamel, cut gemstones, glass and other materials.

item.title: Actress Michelle Chen poses for fashion magazine
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Actress Michelle Chen poses for fashion magazine

item.title: 54th Golden Horse Awards concludes in Taipe
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Chinese actor Tu Men kisses actress Kara Wai's hand with their awards for media for Best Leading Actor and Actress respectively at the 54th Golden Horse Awards in Taipei city, China, Nov 25, 2017. The Golden Horse awards are the Chinese-language film industry's biggest annual events.

item.title: Kang's solo show in Beijing
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: In one of Beijing-based ink painter Kang Chunhui's works, titled Respect, she depicts in the middle of the paper a nest above which rests a mother bird and below which, there are three of her children who are wide opening their mouths.

item.title: Zao Wou-ki painting sets record at auction
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The oil painting 29.01.64, created by Chinese-French painter Zao Wou-ki incorporating the kuangcao (wild cursive) style of Chinese calligraphy, fetched HK$ 202.6 million ($26 million) at a Christie's auction Saturday night.

item.title: Wu Guanzhong's works go under the hammer in December
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The beauty of the area along the Fuchun River, in central Zhejiang province of East China, has been the subject of many artists, the best known of them being the 14th-century ink painting Dwelling on the Fuchun Mountain.

item.title: Hip-hop hero
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Sichuan rapper and winner of online reality show donates prize money of 1 million yuan to promote music education in his hometown.

item.title: Chinese pianist tells range of stories in music
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Zhang Haochen, the youngest gold medal winner of the Van Cliburn International Piano Competition, made his Carnegie Hall solo debut on Nov 18 in New York, treating the audience to a wide-ranging program of classics.

item.title: Chinese TV shows win foreign fans
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: With the fantasy series Tribes and Empires: Story of Prophecy soon to be aired in South Korea, the United States and Canada, China will have a new hit to be fancied by foreign audiences.

item.title: Exhibition of contemporary glass art held in E China's Hangzhou
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Object of Light, an exhibition of contemporary glass art, opened here on Saturday, highlighting around 90 pieces of artwork by some 30 artists from home and abroad.

item.title: Relics to be restored in Beijing's Tongzhou
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: A total of 236 cultural relics in Beijing's Tongzhou district, where a new government center is under construction, will be restored within the next three years, authorities said Wednesday.

item.title: People of Miao ethnic group celebrate traditional New Year festival
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: People of Miao ethnic group dance to celebrate their traditional New Year festival in Maniao village of Danzhai county in Qiandongnan Miao and Dong autonomous prefecture, Southwest China's Guizhou province, Nov 25, 2017.

item.title: On his majesty's service, and at his mercy

item.title: Men on a mission
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Jesuits who arrived in China to preach the gospel between the 16th and 19th centuries left an indelible mark on the country, and it was there that they spent the rest of their natural lives.

item.title: Inspired by Chinese ink
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: An installation by artist Ma Wen on display in Beijing pays tribute to what has been the main medium of expression and communication for centuries in China.

item.title: Young pianist draws inspiration from Rachmaninoff
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: In 1897, Russian composer Sergei Rachmaninoff's Symphony No.1 in D Minor was poorly received at its premier in St. Petersburg. The critical failure, as well as his relationship problems, made the composer doubt himself.

item.title: Rain of light
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: A stunning piece of architecture in its own right, the newly opened Louvre Abu Dhabi is already proving itself as a cultural oasis to rival any museum in the world.

item.title: For art's sake: France, China deepen cultural heritage ties
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: As a national symbol of France, the Palace of Versailles on the outskirts of Paris, also known as the Chateau de Versailles, is actively extending its global horizons in a bid to enhance its international standing.

item.title: Beijing artist Kang Chunhui holds solo exhibition
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: In one of Beijing-based ink painter Kang Chunhui's works, titled Respect, she depicts in the middle of the paper a nest above which rests a mother bird and below which, there are three of her chicks with their mouths wide open.

item.title: Oracle bones added to UNESCO world memory register
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Chinese oracle-bone inscriptions were recently included in the UNESCO Memory of the World Register.

item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Song Changhong is the master of a kite-making craft which traces its roots back to the Song Dynasty (960-1279).

item.title: China cultural center opens in Sofia
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Another China cultural center found a home in Sofia, Bulgaria. The opening ceremony of the Sofia China Cultural Center was held in the city on Nov 23.

item.title: Guangling paper-cutting wins worldwide recognition
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Chinese paper cutting is one of the oldest handicrafts in China. It varies from animals and plants to the vivid scenes of our everyday lives.

item.title: Students create leaf art of 24 Solar Terms
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Students Li Wei and Li Ling from Liaocheng University in Shandong province created a series of leaf art using acrylic paint.

item.title: Iconic moments from Victoria's Secret Fashion Show
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The annual Victoria's Secret Fashion show has just concluded this month. As usual, it has generated animated discussions on social media platforms at home and abroad.

item.title: Author Weir takes readers to the moon in Artemis
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Andy Weir, author of The Martian, takes readers to another desolate world, but instead of the Red Planet it's the moon, in his new novel, Artemis.

item.title: Book Boom
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The 5th China Shanghai International Children's Book Fair attested to growing interest in Chinese publications from local readers and international publishers alike.

item.title: Chinese classic going strong as opera
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: As a Canadian classical music impresario, Wray Armstrong might cut an unlikely figure in the projection of China's soft power.

item.title: Singer launches charity project
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Chinese singer Zhou Yanhong recently joined dozens of celebrities at Beijing's Shijia Primary School to unveil a charity project to help left-behind children and underprivileged college students.

item.title: Meet the musician preserving one of Chinaâs most unique sounds
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: One of the most unique and high-pitched sounds often heard in traditional Chinese music comes from the suona, an instrument one musician is trying to preserve. Zhou Dongchao is a suona master in Beijing who has been playing the instrument for nearly 50 years.

item.title: Visually-impaired students touch history with their senses
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: On Wednesday, more than 200 teenagers from the Zhejiang Provincial School for Visually-Impaired Students visited Zhejiang Provincial Museum in Hangzhou, where they tried to feel the history of the country, led by their own senses and a curiosity as precious as the antiques.

item.title: Sharing economy turns new page with books
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: From sharing bicycle to sharing car, China's sharing economy has now swept into the book industry.

item.title: Inheriting the ancient Shouzhou kiln in Anhui
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Huainan city in East China's Anhui province is known for the Shouzhou kiln, one of the five great kilns of ancient China's Tang Dynasty (618-907).

item.title: Christie's to auction Picasso, Matisse from David Rockerfeller's collection
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: A collection of Peggy and David Rockefeller that shows the couple's wide-ranging interest in art and antiquity will be auctioned in New York in 2018.

item.title: Hainan plans poetry event to mark anniversary
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: To celebrate the 30th anniversary of Hainan province and its status as one of the five special economic zones in China, a poetry festival has been planned.

item.title: Hong Kong scholar's works on show at Beijing exhibition
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Noted Hong Kong-based scholar Jao Tsung-i (Rao Zongyi), 100, was named after Zhou Dunyi, an 11th-century philosopher, by his father who hoped that he could be someday as learned as Zhou.

item.title: Wang Tianmin showcases new artworks
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Beijing-based artist Wang Tianmin recently published an album that catalogs the development of his ink paintings and calligraphic pieces. It traces his experiments with a freehand style of ink-brush painting and his accomplishments in handling the various styles of traditional Chinese writing.

item.title: Architectural photo award winners go on show
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The winning work and runners-up at this year's Architectural Photography Awards, presented on Nov 18 in Berlin, are being exhibited at Beijing's True Space gallery through Dec 25.

item.title: Stone carving competition held in C China
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: A contestant works on a stone sculpture during a competition of stone carving skills in Wuhan, capital of Central China's Hubei province, Nov 22, 2017.

item.title: American Melodies
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The Last of the Mohicans - the soundtrack of the 1992 film by the same title, starring Daniel Day-Lewis - was composed by Trevor Jones and Randy Edelman.

item.title: Buzz among film buffs as Chinese thriller shines at Tokyo festival
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Director Dong Yue's first feature film, The Looming Storm, recently won two top awards - for best actor and best artistic contribution - at the 30th Tokyo International Film Festival, leading to a buzz among crime noir fans.

item.author: 中国日报-文化

item.title: Dong Chorus
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: As night falls, a young man takes a wooden ladder and leans it against a closed window on the second floor of a house. Playing a guitar-like instrument, he sings romantic lyrics as his beloved looks on from the other side of the window.

item.title: EU-China Literary Festival promotes cultural communications
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The first EU-China Literary Festival was held in Beijing on Tuesday. By inviting 28 award-winning authors from the European Union and China, the festival set out to promote cultural exchanges between the two sides and give insights into the lives, works, and unique character of their literary traditions.

item.title: Miao people celebrates traditional New Year festival
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: People of the Miao ethnic group dance during a fair to celebrate the traditional New Year Festival in Wubao village of Jianhe county in Qiandongnan Miao and Dong autonomous prefecture, Southwest China's Guizhou province, Nov 20, 2017.

item.title: Minor Snow: A forerunner of winter chills
item.author: 中国日报-文化

item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Major bonsai artworks from bonsai masters such as Pan Zhonglian, a pioneer of the Zhejiang-style bonsai, are on show at Guozhuang in Hangzhou city.

item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The 2017 Fifth Hubei Fine Arts Festival and also Third Hubei International Contemporary Arts Festival is currently underway at the Wanlin Art Museum in Wuhan University.

item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: More than 200 art pieces by famous Chinese artists in modern times are being showcased at the the National Art Museum of China in Beijing.

item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The first floor of the Chengdu Museum in the heart of Chengdu, capital of Southwest China's Sichuan province, has been thronged with visitors admiring the famous piece Water Lilies by French impressionist painter Claude Monet since it went on display Sept 27.

item.description: US band Weezer doesn't always get the credit it deserves as it makes everything look so easy.

item.title: Art draws the Singer's attention
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: About two years ago, Singaporean singer-songwriter Stefanie Sun picked up painting. It was part of the national movement, called SkillsFuture, which provides Singaporeans aged 25 and above with an initial $500 of SkillsFuture Credit to develop their potential.

item.title: Han Song's sci-fi novel wins top award
item.author: 中国日报-文化

item.description: A familiar face in many Hong Kong comedies, veteran actor Eric Tsang returns to his comfort zone with his role in the upcoming feature Kill Me Please.

item.description: For those who can afford it, owning expensive works of art is often viewed as a showcase for their wealth and an investment that may bring enormous returns.

item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: As an oil painter who is also a fan of Ganju Opera, Yu Xiaofei reveals the charm of Chinese traditional culture using Western painting techniques.

item.title: Painter places lacquer at heart of his creations
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Lacquer was for centuries an important material used to craft artistic objects in the daily lives of the ancient Chinese. A coating of the material brings a shiny finish to an object that has already been carved, painted or inlaid with decorative materials.

item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The Tianqiao Performing Arts Center celebrated its second anniversary in Beijing.

item.description: Geng Fawang, a nearly 70-year-old man living in Xinzheng city in Henan province, is part of the 13th generation of the Geng family, well-known for incense-making expertise.

item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Sunday is the first day of the Kongpo New Year in the Nyingchi area according to the Tibetan calendar. In observing New Year customs, locals would clean their homes, dress up in festive clothes, set off fireworks and have fun.

item.title: Chinese and Mauritian watercolor paintings on display in Mauritius
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: A new exhibition opened Friday at the China Cultural Center in Mauritius to promote artistic exchanges between Chinese and Mauritian artists and enhance cultural cooperation between the two nations.

item.title: China's box office earning crosses $7.5b this year
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: China's box office exceeded 50 billion yuan ($7.5 billion) Monday, the first year the country has broken the 50-billion mark, according to the State Administration of Press, Publication, Radio, Film and Television (SAPPRFT).

item.title: Over 200 artists create street paintings in Houston
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: HOUSTON - More than 200 artists from around the world took part in this year's Houston Via Colori, one of the largest and most well-known art festivals in the city in the US state of Texas. Now in its 12th year, Via Colori saw an attendance of around 30,000 people, three stages of live music, an array of local food and beverage vendors, and award-winning street painters.

item.title: Painter places lacquer at heart of his creations
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Lacquer was for centuries an important material used to craft artistic objects in the daily lives of the ancient Chinese. A coating of the material brings a shiny finish to an object that has already been carved, painted or inlaid with decorative materials.

item.title: Ripe for return
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: In 2000, a spherical 16th-century polychrome jar which once adorned a Chinese emperors' palace sold for HK$44 million ($5.64 million) at a Hong Kong auction, establishing a world record for Chinese porcelain at the time. The jar had sound provenance, being once owned by Hu Huichun, an eminent Shanghai connoisseur of Chinese art during the 20th century. The victorious bidder was Robert Tsao, a Taiwan entrepreneur and noted collector of Chinese art. The bidding was fierce and Tsao's competitors included London-based Guiseppe Eskenazi, one of the most important Chinese art dealers in the world.

item.title: Master of the big picture
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Pan Gongkai, 70, the son of modern ink painting master Pan Tianshou (1897-1971) is a busy man these days.

item.title: Spectacular water drum dance enthralls tourists in SW China
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Water drum dance is a unique dance performed by the Miao ethnic group during their sacrificial offering ceremony. All performers were dressed in traditional costumes of the Miao ethnic group, dancing to the drumbeat around a giant wooden drum in the center of the square.

item.title: Guangxi claims world's largest bronze drum
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: A giant bronze drum is seen in Huanjiang Maonan autonomous county, Southwest China's Guangxi Zhuang autonomous region, Nov 19, 2017.

item.title: Chinese retiree becomes model ship master
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Zhao Jinlong assembles an ancient ship model in Jilin, Northeast China's Jilin province, Nov 18, 2017. Zhao Jinlong started to made ancient ship models when he got a set of drawings of the English ship The Mayflower in the 1990s, and he has successfully made nearly 20 models since then among which the longest one reaches 1.5 meters long.

item.title: Int'l pattern design contest held in E China's Zhejiang
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: A visitor views pattern design works at Zhejiang Art Museum in Hangzhou, East China's Zhejiang province, Nov 19, 2017. The 2017 international creative pattern design competition concluded here on Sunday. A total of 30 works won the prizes of the competition.

item.author: 中国日报-文化

item.description: The Tianqiao Performing Arts Center celebrated its second anniversary in Beijing on Nov 19, 2017.

item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: An exhibition of folk handicrafts is now being held at the Cultural Heritage and Communication Research Center of the Communication University Of China in Beijing.

item.title: Nearly 70 countries, organizations confirmed to join Beijing expo 2019

The special exhibition on ancient book restoration will last till Nov 23.

item.author: 中国日报-文化

item.title: China's music market now marching to a new beat
item.author: 中国日报-文化

item.description: On a chilly autumn night in Beijing, Ye Bei and a group of musician friends meet to celebrate Ye's latest album with a concert

item.author: 中国日报-文化

item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: An actress of traditional Chinese Qinqiang Opera prepares for performance at the Yisu Theater, a century-old theater in Xi'an, Northwest China's Shaanxi province, on Nov 15, 2017. item.title: Chinese and German filmmakers aim to learn from each other
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Imagine this, you are a member of a jury, and sitting in the center of the court room is a fighter pilot who is on trial for murder. He has shot down a hijacked civilian jet, killing 164 passengers on board, to stop Islamic terrorists from crashing it into a stadium with 70,000 civilians.

item.title: Tai chi goddess: Young master teaches ancient martial arts online
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The Chinese martial art form of tai chi is centuries old. And the Yang-style is the most popular in the world.

item.title: Wheat straw becomes art
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: 67-year-old Zhang Chongsheng turns wheat straw into works of art in Jinan, East China's Shandong province. Zhang has created art from straw for more than 40 years. Due to his artistic achievements, Zhang was certified as a "folk master of arts and crafts" and straw art was listed as intangible cultural heritage in Jinan.

item.title: Tencent has plenty in store for online video fans
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Fans of King of Glory, Tencent Holding Ltd's top-grossing mobile game, will have a new pastime in Decemberï¼the game-adapted variety show Wang Zhe Chu Ji (The Ace Valor), where 20 Chinese players in parallel with the game characters will have "five versus five" battles.

item.title: Li Yundi joins a classical force
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Chinese pianist teams up with German orchestra Staatskapelle Dresden to round off China tour.

item.title: Fans can look forward to Liu Jian's latest animation film
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Film director Liu Jian is by no means talkative. Liu, who was invited to the opening ceremony of the fifth Festival of German Cinema in China in Beijing on Friday as chief guest, spoke just five sentences in his opening speech.

item.title: Seeking&nbsp;to collaborate
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Australian movie and TV producers unveil projects combining Chinese and Australian elements. Xu Fan reports.

item.title: Are traditional martial arts losing their luster?
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: A thin, bony guy walked into a building, each story heavily guarded. On the first floor alone there were five martial arts masters, ranging from Olympic boxing champion Zou Shiming to world-famous action movie star Tony Jaa.

item.title: China-Russia music festival to debut in May
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The first China-Russia Music Festival will be held in Beijing in May, the festival's initiator, Russian actress Anikieva Selena, announced at the Russian embassy in Beijing on Monday.

item.title: Hit Chinese novel as online series
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Upcoming Chinese series Tribes and Empires: Storm of Prophecy, adapted from the hit novel of the same name, will run on the streaming sites Youku, iQiyi and Tencent from Tuesday.

item.title: Exquisite screens keep out the cold in winter
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: In celebration of winter, the Palace Museum in Beijing has shared a range of photos featuring its collection of Chinese pingfeng, or screens, with netizens on the micro blog Sina Weibo.

item.title: Cute cats pose like Chinese kung fu fighters
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: talented photographer Hisakata Hiroyuki has managed to snap a group of Mortal Tom cats in a variety of high-flying kung fu poses. Using his own rapid-fire reactions, Hiroyuki has captured the lovable cats flying through the air, their legs and paws outstretched like something out of an action movie.

item.title: 'Experience China 2017: A Glimpse of West China' held in Los Angeles
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Artists perform during the event Experience China 2017 -- A Glimpse of West China held in Los Angeles, the United States, Nov 13, 2017.

item.title: China International Ballet Season underway in Beijing
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Good news for lovers of ballet in Beijing! The Third China International Ballet Season is now underway in the Chinese capital. The opening gala at the Tianqiao Theater involved stars from several global ballet companies.

item.title: Playlist
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: playlist

item.title: Mexican IT students in China for training
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: MEXICO CITY - Ten Mexican university students traveled to China in early November to receive two weeks of information technology training, Mexico's communications and transport ministry said.

item.title: Boost for young musicians
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: A concert gala of the second China Youth Music Competition, based on the format of a contest in Germany, has just been staged in Beijing.

item.title: Porcelain beads in Jingdezhen
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: As the first maker of porcelain jewelry in Jingdezhen, the company has been dedicated to turning ceramic residues into exquisite beadwork. It takes 10 years for the company to improve manufacturing techniques and produce glossy beads with high hardness. Jingdezhen, or Jingde town, formerly known as the "Porcelain Capital" of China, is one of China's most famous cultural and historic cities. [Photo/Chinanews.com]

item.title: Artist paints her dreams, plants in vibrant spring colors
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The colors of spring can be found once again. They are in painter Jiang Jing's art, featured at her recent exhibition titled Lyric Suite.

item.title: National carving vocational skill contest held in E China's Jiangxi
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: A participant works on a root sculpture at the 2017 Chinese skill competition - national carving vocational skill contest final - in Yujiang county, East China's Jiangxi province, Nov 13, 2017.

item.title: Amazon to produce 'Lord of the Rings' television series
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Amazon has bought the global television rights to The Lord of the Rings, the company announced Monday, in what may be its biggest and most expensive move yet to draw viewers to its streaming and shopping club Prime.

item.title: Selections from Summer Palace to be exhibited in US
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Photo taken on Nov 10, 2017 shows a media preview of the "Empress Dowager Cixi: Selections from the Summer Palace" exhibition in the United States.

item.title: Chinese hip-hop singers make UK debut in Manchester
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: As hip-hop catches on in China, three of its most hyped home-grown rap singers are making their UK debut in Manchester.

item.title: Heritage parks planned for Great Wall
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Beijing will actively direct its efforts toward creating national parks along the Great Wall to highlight its tourism brand, according to authorities attending the 7th Beijing Tourism Development Forum on Nov 11.

item.title: Four designers create dress from 5,888 real leaves
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: As trees shed their leaves in South China, four designers chose 5,888 real leaves to create an amazing 2-meter dress. The handmade leave dress was displayed by a model at Red Leaves Festival in Shampoola Forest Resort in Qingyuan city, South China's Guangdong province, on November 12.

item.title: Awards, rewards for best online jokers
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Sina Weibo, one of China's largest social networks, announced it would team up with the Beijing-based firm Joy Entertainment to honor the best online jokes in Beijing on Tuesday.

item.title: Confucius Institute helps good trade links between UK, China
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: A leading British academic on Monday defended the award-winning Confucius Institute at the University of Sheffield, saying it is key to helping business in northern England create trade with China.

item.title: Top 10 cultural relics from Haihunhou tomb
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The top 10 cultural relics of Haihunhou ruins in Nanchang city were revealed recently, with Emperor Liu He's jade seal, chime and gold wares being selected.

item.title: College students miniature Palace Museum on a wooden board
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Six undergraduates of Dalian Minzu University are building a wooden miniature of the Palace Museum at a scale of 1:1000.

item.title: 1st China craft boutique biennial exhibition held in E China
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The first China Craft Boutique Biennial Exhibition is being held at the Ningbo Museum of Art in Ningbo city, East China's Zhejiang province, from Nov 10 to 14, 2017.

item.title: Winners for The Most Beautiful Books of China 2017 competition revealed
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Twenty-five books have emerged as the winners of The Most Beautiful Books of China 2017 competition.

item.title: A glimpse of the past: Int'l students tour Xi'an
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: International students from Peking University's Yenching Academy look at a Terracotta Warrior in Emperor Qinshihuang's Mausoleum Site Museum in Xi'an, Northwest China's Shaanxi province, Nov 13, 2017.

item.title: 2,400-year-old horse and chariot pit unearthed in China
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Archaeologists work at the No. 3 Horse and Chariot Pit, one of a cluster of tombs belonging to noble families of the Zheng State.

item.title: US first lady Melania Trump visits Beijing Zoo
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: US first lady Melania Trump smiles with children as she visits Beijing Zoo in Beijing, China, Nov 10, 2017.

item.title: Xi, Peng host Trump family for tea in Palace Museum
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping and his wife Peng Liyuan accompany visiting US President Donald Trump and his wife Melania on a tour in Beijing's Palace Museum, Nov 8, 2017.

item.title: Presidential couples spend day at Palace Museum
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Editor's note: China provided a "state visit-plus" reception for US President Donald Trump on Wednesday, with special arrangements for his arrival in Beijing. China Daily brings you a detailed account of the events and exchanges between President Xi Jinping and his US counterpart.

item.title: Memorable moments cement China-US ties
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: China continued its "state visit-plus" reception for US President Donald Trump on Thursday, with special arrangements at a number of official events in Beijing.

item.title: German film festival tours China
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The 5th Festival of German Cinema in China, a key annual Sino-German cultural communications event, raised its curtain in Beijing on Nov 10.

item.title: Tribute to master of rural landscapes
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Echoes of Canglang, an ongoing exhibition in Beijing, looks at Luo Erchun's artistic evolution over six decades.

item.title: Natural ability
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Inspired by the natural beauty of Huangshan Mountain, Liu Haisu set out to modernize Chinese art through innovation and education.

item.title: Qing clothing highlights connection with faith
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The parasol, golden fish, conch shell, lotus, vase, dharma wheel, victory banner and "eternal knots" are considered auspicious in Buddhism.

item.title: Display of modern Chinese ink masters
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: If you want to better understand an ink painter's personality, then albums and handscrolls are probably most revealing about an artist, because their intimate style was designed to be appreciated among friends.

item.title: Artist lets wood speak for itself in Beijing show
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Zhou Ning, an artist who lives in rural China, is presenting his wood art in Beijing.

item.title: Chinese Cultural Week kicks off in Jordan's Zarqa
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The activities of the Chinese Cultural Week started in Jordan's Zarqa governorate on Sunday.

item.title: Brussels exhibition showcases traditional Chinese lifestyles and culture
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: An exhibition showcasing traditional Chinese culture and ways of living is being held at the China Cultural Center in Brussels.

item.title: President Xi inaugurates Vietnam-China Friendship Palace
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Visiting Chinese President Xi Jinping attended the inauguration and handover ceremony of Vietnam-China Friendship Palace in Hanoi on Sunday. The palace, occupying an area of 13,800 square meters, is a signature China-sponsored project in Vietnam.

item.title: Young people continue the art of 'painting with fire'
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Li Yanjie, a post-90s man, has loved pyrography for a long time. He is dedicated to improving and promoting pyrography, opening the only pyrography art shop in Zhengzhou, Central China's Henan province. Li believes that through his efforts, pyrography will transform from a marginal handicraft into a popular one.

item.title: Selections from Summer Palace to be exhibited in US
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The"Empress Dowager Cixi: Selections from the Summer Palace" exhibition  will kick off on Nov 12 and last till Mar 11, 2018 in the United States.

item.title: Student art works displayed in Changsha, C China
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: An art exhibition was held at Changsha Normal University on Saturday, showcasing art creations by students from more than 30 universities and colleges.

item.title: From trash to art: 'Low-carbon' sculptures go on display in Beijing
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: An art space, named Beijing Low Carbon Sculpture Garden, opened on Nov 7, featuring 24 pieces made from "low-carbon" material.

item.title: Hollywood and China see a future for co-productions
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: In response to the agreement between China and the United States to encourage cross-cultural, people-to-people exchanges, U.S. and Chinese filmmakers are gearing up to jump into the fray.

item.title: Sugar paintings leave a sweet taste
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Every day, a stall selling sugar paintings in a shopping mall in Changchun, capital of Jilin province, attracts many visitors and buyers.

item.title: 2017 MTV European Music Awards opens in London
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Chinese singer Huo Zun wins the award of Best Chinese Artist at the MTV European Music Awards 2017 in London, Sunday, Nov 12, 2017.

item.title: Tianjin festival offers a feast for city's opera fans
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: In 1978, the China National Opera House staged Giuseppe Verdi's opera La Traviata, which was conducted by Zheng Xiaoying in Tianjin.

item.title: Literary attraction
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Sichuan's capital is composing a new chapter in developing its tourism by commemorating the ancient luminary poet Du Fu. Huang Zhiling reports in Chengdu.

item.title: Liverpool cements cultural status with Terracotta Warriors
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The British football city of Liverpool has set itself the goal of becoming a top cultural destination with the help of China's iconic Terracotta Warriors, in a move likely to boost visitor numbers to the city.

item.title: Music with meaning
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Fang Qiong, a renowned singer and professor at the Shanghai Conservatory of Music, presents a concert in Beijing, with ancient poetry in focus.

item.title: Star-studded 2017 Tmall Double Eleven Global Shopping Festival gala show
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Highlights of 2017 Tmall Double Eleven Global Shopping Festival gala show

item.title: The web we weave
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Argentinian artist Tomas Saraceno imagines our human future through spider research.

item.title: Chinese comedy film 'Never Say Die' wins box office success
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Chinese films are increasingly enjoying box office success. The Chinese comedy Never Say Die has brought in an impressive $326 million worldwide to date, marking another triumph after Wolf Warriors II piled up $877 million.

item.title: Building a music bridge between China, US
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: A euphonic flow of traditional Chinese music Jasmine flower leaked out of a classroom of the Valparaiso University in the northwestern part of the US state of Indiana. A Chinese yangqin teacher was instructing a US student on the traditional Chinese musical instrument, a hammered dulcimer.

item.title: Chinese musicians bring music to Philadelphia communities
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Long-standing strategic partnership hopes to give US citizens access to young Chinese artists playing their music and Western works.

item.title: What's on in Beijing (Nov 11-17)
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Discover the best things to do in Beijing with our weekly roundup of art and exhibitions, music and performances, and trending activities to do around town.

item.title: Ancient house with scripture halls of Tibetan Buddhism seen in Sichuan
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Photo taken on Nov 8, 2017 shows the kitchen in the ancient scripture hall of Zimai family in Zilong village of Daocheng county, Southwest China's Sichuan province.

item.title: Li Quan to play Blue Note Beijing
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Chinese singer-songwriter Li Quan will play a show at Blue Note Beijing on Nov 16, with his band, which features jazz pianist Huang Jianyi, double bass player Ren Yuqing, trumpet player Hu Danfeng, saxophonist Li Shihai and percussionist An Yu. Li will perform a remix of his pop hits in a jazz style.

item.title: Chinese patissier wins gold in world cake competition
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: A Chinese style fondant cake created by patissier Zhou Yi recently won a big round of applause as well as the First International Best prize at Cake International, the world's largest cake competition held in the UK.

item.title: Dragon boat racing sees growing popularity around the globe
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The 13th World Dragon Boat Racing Championships ended on Oct 22 in Kunming, Yunnan province, after around 2,800 athletes from over 20 countries took part in five days of competitive racing.

item.title: French haute couture mosaics add unique touch to floor tiles
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Get to know David Dalichoux, the French creator of haute couture floor tiles.

item.title: Comedy film's release timed for Singles Day
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The comedy film Two Wrongs Make A Right opens to the public in China's mainland today.

item.title: From 2001 to 2017, memories of 'Meteor Garden'
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: On August 12, 2001, a mega-hit Taiwanese drama called Meteor Garden was born.

item.title: Chinese government gives push to music industry
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: During the fourth Music Industry Forum held on Nov 3, the State Administration of Press, Publication, Radio, Film and Television claimed a series of promotions will be given to the Chinese music industry in the future.

item.title: Modern lacquer works go on display in Beijing
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: About 60 pieces of lacquer work from the Sichuan Fine Arts Institute will be showcased at the National Art Museum of China in Beijing on Nov 29.

item.title: Hangzhou exhibits ceramic pieces worldwide
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The China exhibition of the Gyeonggi International Ceramics Biennale is ongoing at Nansong Gallery on the Royal Street of the Southern Song Dynasty (1127-1279) in Hangzhou city, which will run through Nov 30.

item.title: US organ inspires musicians to enhance cultural ties
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: With her fingers dancing along a four-tier keyboard and her head swaying with the rhythm, Fang Site sits in front of a four-storey high pipe organ, the audience watching her in awe.

item.title: Shanghai Museum to hold China's largest fresco exhibition
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Chinese restorers have selected 89 original fresco paintings, some centuries old, for what will be the largest fresco exhibition to be held in China.

item.title: Prescient author now rules the roost
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Writer who predicted the rise of China as a global power argues country has proved its model of governance works.

item.title: Royal baths shed light on Qin rulers
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Three royal baths have been unearthed among the ruins of an ancient city in Xi'an, Shaanxi province, archaeologists said.

item.title: Louis Cha's acclaimed trilogy to be translated into English
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The English translation of Louis Cha's martial arts trilogy will be published in February.

item.title: US art historian in love with Dunhuang
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The 74-year-old US art historian Mimi Gates has passionately participated in the conservation of the Dunhuang relics for years.

item.title: Delicate frescos found in deserted temple in E China
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Delicate frescos have been discovered recently in a deserted ancient temple within a pig farm in Zhangyilang village in the Zhangqiu district of Jinan, capital of East China's Shandong province.

item.title: Miao people gather at traditional festival to celebrate harvest
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Women of Miao ethnic group gather at a traditional festival to celebrate harvest and pray for good fortune next year in Gaotong village of Rongjiang county, Southwest China's Guizhou province, Nov 8, 2017.

item.title: Stone carving reproduces 'China's Mona Lisa'
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: A stone carving of the masterpiece Along the River During the Qingming Festival is a highlight of the 2017 China(Huian) International Engraving Expo opens today in Huian county, Quanzhou, East China's Fujian province. Completed by two masters of stone carving, the sculpture represents a high level of the Huian engraving school, as leaves and lines on twisted ropes are clearly visible.

item.title: Chinese, French artists create graffiti artworks in Beijing
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: Hip Hop artists from China and France created graffiti artworks together during a cultural festival of "Paris Hip Hop" in Beijing.

item.title: 'Miss Julie' wins top award at drama festival for Beijing colleges
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: The closing ceremony of the 6th Beijing College Students Drama Festival was held at the Central Academy of Drama in Beijing on Nov 7.

item.title: Trump's granddaughter wins Chinese hearts, again
item.author: 中国日报-文化
item.description: A video recording showing Arabella Kushner, US President Donald Trump's granddaughter, singing Chinese songs and reciting Chinese literary classics caused another internet sensation in China Wednesday night.
